channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Iran effectively closes Strait of Hormuz as U.S.-Israel strikes continue
item.description: Iran has effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz as the U.S. and Israel continue airstrikes in the country, triggering what's being described as the "largest oil disruption in history."
item.content:encoded: Iran has effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz as the U.S. and Israel continue airstrikes in the country, triggering what's being described as the "largest oil disruption in history."
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Sen. Chris Murphy, D-Conn., says Trump's plans for the war in Iran are 'incoherent'
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Democratic Sen. Chris Murphy of Connecticut, who says the Trump administration's war plans for Iran are "incoherent and incomplete."
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Democratic Sen. Chris Murphy of Connecticut, who says the Trump administration's war plans for Iran are "incoherent and incomplete."
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: The Trump administration's crackdown on immigrant truckers shifts into higher gear
item.description: The White House wants tougher rules for commercial licenses after several high-profile crashes involving foreign-born drivers. But critics say that would do little to make the nation's roads safer.
item.content:encoded:
The White House wants tougher rules for commercial licenses after several high-profile crashes involving foreign-born drivers. But critics say that would do little to make the nation's roads safer.

(Image credit: Mario Tama)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: U.S.-Israel strikes continue as Iran effectively closes Strait of Hormuz, Trump ran on lower gas prices, but the Iran war challenges that, probe points to U.S. being at fault for Iran school strike.
item.content:encoded: U.S.-Israel strikes continue as Iran effectively closes Strait of Hormuz, Trump ran on lower gas prices, but the Iran war challenges that, probe points to U.S. being at fault for Iran school strike.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Rep. Kevin Kiley talks about why he switched his party affiliation
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Rep. Kevin Kiley of California about changing his political party affiliation from Republican to independent.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Rep. Kevin Kiley of California about changing his political party affiliation from Republican to independent.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: DOJ's program to restore gun rights to felons raises questions about transparency
item.description: The Department of Justice has quietly restarted a decades-dormant program to restore gun rights to felons. One name on the list is raising questions about transparency.
item.content:encoded: The Department of Justice has quietly restarted a decades-dormant program to restore gun rights to felons. One name on the list is raising questions about transparency.
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz

item.title: Trump ran on lowering gas prices. The war with Iran is challenging that promise
item.description: President Trump made lowering gas prices the centerpiece of his affordability agenda. He now faces the political perils of the war with Iran, as Middle East energy infrastructure is disrupted.
item.content:encoded: President Trump made lowering gas prices the centerpiece of his affordability agenda. He now faces the political perils of the war with Iran, as Middle East energy infrastructure is disrupted.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: How the release of 400 million barrels of oil will affect the global energy market
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with energy analyst John Kilduff about the global release of more than 400 million barrels of oil, and its impact on global energy markets and U.S. gas prices.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with energy analyst John Kilduff about the global release of more than 400 million barrels of oil, and its impact on global energy markets and U.S. gas prices.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Blue cities in red states debate over how to respond to efforts to resist ICE
item.description: While cities in blue states like Minnesota and California resist ICE enforcement, some Democrat-led cities in red states, like Austin, Texas, are in a heated debate over how to respond.
item.content:encoded: While cities in blue states like Minnesota and California resist ICE enforcement, some Democrat-led cities in red states, like Austin, Texas, are in a heated debate over how to respond.
item.dc:creator: Mose Buchele

item.title: To stop Africa from hemorrhaging local talent, scientist creates drug discovery lab
item.description: A Zambian scientist is on a quest to prevent brain drain from Africa so he's established a state-of-the-art drug discovery lab in South Africa.
item.content:encoded: A Zambian scientist is on a quest to prevent brain drain from Africa so he's established a state-of-the-art drug discovery lab in South Africa.
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel
